days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant neighborhoods

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Arrive at this chic hotel in Shinjuku and drop your bags. Check out the famous Godzilla head on the roof!

timeline.title: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Relax in this beautiful park, featuring traditional Japanese, English, and French gardens. Perfect for relief from the city's hustle.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor a unique dining experience at this famous ramen chain known for its individual booths and customizable ramen.

timeline.title: Explore Shibuya Crossing
timeline.detail: Witness the iconic scramble crossing at Shibuya. A must-see for visitors; the energy and crowds are electrifying!

timeline.title: Relax at the hotel bar
timeline.detail: Wind down at the hotel's bar and enjoy a cocktail while reflecting on your first day in Tokyo.

localTips: Purchase a prepaid Suica card at the airport for convenient transportation.
Most restaurants in Shinjuku have a non-smoking section; ask for it if needed.
Explore the back streets for the best local eateries.
Be prepared for cash transactions; not all places accept credit cards.
Use Google Maps for public transit navigation—it works great in Tokyo.

days.title: Tokyo Sightseeing
days.subtitle: Cultural immersion and delicious food

timeline.title: Breakfast at Denny's
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast at this Japanese Denny's, known for its unique menu blends both Western and Japanese options.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple in Asakusa, famous for the iconic Kaminarimon Gate. Immerse in the spiritual atmosphere.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Imbiss
timeline.detail: Try authentic Japanese street food at this bustling spot nearby Senso-ji. Don't miss the yakitori and taiyaki!

timeline.title: Tokyo Skytree Observation Deck
timeline.detail: Experience bird's eye views of Tokyo from the 350m high observation deck. Perfect for panoramic photos.

timeline.title: Dinner at Uobei Shibuya Dogenzaka
timeline.detail: Indulge in a speedy sushi experience at this conveyor belt sushi chain, where food comes to you at high speed via an automated system.

timeline.title: Evening stroll at Ueno Park
timeline.detail: Relax in Ueno Park, wandering the serene paths and enjoying the scenery or visiting a museum if you're still feeling curious.

localTips: Buy a Skytree ticket online to skip the lines.
Visit Asakusa during the morning for fewer crowds.
Consider strolling through nearby Nakamise-dori for traditional snacks.
Check for seasonal festivals or events at temples.
Take Japan Rail to get a feel for local commute.

days.title: Last Day Adventures
days.subtitle: Winding down your Tokyo experience

timeline.title: Breakfast at the hotel
timeline.detail: Savor a buffet-style breakfast that includes both Western and Japanese options for a balanced start to your final day.

timeline.title: Meiji Shrine visit
timeline.detail: Explore this serene Shinto shrine located in a forested area, offering a peaceful escape from the city.

timeline.title: Shopping in Harajuku
timeline.detail: Stroll through the trendy streets of Harajuku, checking out eclectic shops and unique fashion boutiques.

timeline.title: Lunch at Kawaii Monster Cafe
timeline.detail: Experience a vibrant themed cafe that offers an eccentric dining atmosphere. Kids and adults alike will enjoy the unique decorations.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaii Monster Cafe, Harajuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Last minute shopping at Shibuya 109
timeline.detail: Take some time to grab souvenirs and discover trendy fashion at the famous Shibuya 109 department store.

timeline.title: Departure from Tokyo
timeline.detail: Check out from the hotel and head to Narita International Airport for your onward journey.

localTips: Consider luggage forwarding services to relax while exploring on departure day.
Always check store hours; many close early.
Try to experience at least one traditional kaiseki meal in Tokyo if time allows.
Allow plenty of time for airport transfers, as Narita can be far from central Tokyo.
Use the JR Pass if you're planning additional travel.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: Located in the heart of Shinjuku, within walking distance to transportation.

budget.note: Return flight from Singapore to Tokyo
¥18,000 per night at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku

urgent.label: Book entry for Senso-ji Temple
urgent.note: Check for any special event schedules or rituals during your visit.

urgent.label: Reserve Tokyo Skytree tickets
urgent.note: Avoid long waiting times on-site by booking early.
